entry.title: Re: #CoE#BUMA - Item price has not changed in the catalog...
entry.content:
Hello @_JohnL_ 

Thanks for the questiona, The error message you are encountering, "The price for item 00010 has changed in the catalog. Update item price," typically occurs when there is a discrepancy between the price of the item in the external catalog and the price recorded in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system (either in the Purchasing Info Record or the material master).

Here are some steps and considerations to resolve this issue:

1. Verify Price Consistency: Ensure that the price of the material in the external catalog matches the price in the Purchasing Info Record (PIR) or the material master. If they differ, you need to decide which price should be considered correct and update the other accordingly.

2.Synchronize Catalog and SAP System: Regularly synchronize the prices in the external catalog with those in the SAP system to prevent such discrepancies. This can be part of a routine check or automated if your system and the catalog provider support such functionality.

3.BAdI Customization: Since you are using the BAdI MMPUR_OCI_ITEM_TRANSFER_DATA, you can implement additional logic to handle price discrepancies. For example, you could add a check that compares the catalog price and the SAP price. If they differ, the BAdI could automatically update the price in the purchase requisition to match the catalog price, or it could trigger a notification for manual review.

4.User Training and Communication: Ensure that users are aware of the importance of maintaining consistent pricing information between the catalog and the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Training sessions or memos can help emphasize the impact of price discrepancies on the procurement process.

5.Error Handling in BAdI: Enhance the error handling within the BAdI to provide more descriptive and actionable messages. Instead of a generic error, provide guidance on what needs to be done to resolve the issue (e.g., "Please update the price in the Purchasing Info Record to match the external catalog").

6.Testing: After making adjustments, thoroughly test the process to ensure that the error does not recur. Test with different scenarios to cover various price mismatch situations.

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the price discrepancy issue and prevent similar errors in the future when creating purchase requisitions from external catalogs.

Best Regards

Dave

entry.title: Do I Need to Manually Implement UPDATE_ENTITY_LIST in SAP RAP Managed Scenario?
entry.content:
I am working with SAP ABAP RAP in a Managed Scenario to enable upsert functionality for an OData V4 API. I am using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud's Developer Extensibility feature, and the source code was automatically generated through the Generate RAP Service option in Eclipse.

My understanding is that UPDATE_ENTITY_LIST does not need to be manually implemented in a Managed Scenario because the framework should handle create, update, and delete operations automatically when they are defined in the Behavior Definition.

The OData service works perfectly for single entity operations (create, update, and delete). However, I encountered an issue when testing upsert with a Delta Payload PATCH request for a collection of entities:

{"error":{"code":"/IWBEP/CM_V4S_RUN/003","message":"Not implemented - The request cannot be processed; Implement '/IWBEP/IF_V4_DP_ADVANCED~UPDATE_ENTITY_LIST'","@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory":"Provider_Application_Error","innererror":{"ErrorDetails":{"@SAP__common.Application":{"ComponentId":"BC-ESI-ESF-GW","ServiceRepository":"SRVD_A2X","ServiceId":"ZAPI_EAC_TEST_TBL_O4","ServiceVersion":"0001"},"@SAP__common.TransactionId":"9E74C0DFBEB60000E00674B0F7224F8C","@SAP__common.Timestamp":"20241201082256.048968","@SAP__common.ErrorResolution":{"Analysis":"Use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details","Note":"See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)"}}}}}

What I Have Done:

  1. Behavior Definition:

    • I defined the create, update, and delete operations for the entity in my Behavior Definition:

define behavior for ZR_EAC_TEST_TBL
persistent table ZEAC_TEST_TBL
lock master
authorization master( global )
{
field ( mandatory : create ) ApprNo;
field ( readonly : update ) ApprNo;
field ( mandatory : create ) ItemNm;
field ( readonly : update ) ItemNm;

create;
update;
delete;
}

  1. Service Binding:

    • The OData service binding was successfully published, and CRUD operations for single entities work as expected.

  2. Testing Upsert:

    • I tested upsert functionality using a PATCH request with a Delta Payload ("@context": "#$delta") via Postman.
    • Despite defining create and update in the Behavior Definition, I received the above error.

Questions:

  1. Is manual implementation of the UPDATE_ENTITY_LIST method necessary in a Managed Scenario for upsert functionality?

    • If not, why is the framework unable to handle it automatically?

  2. If manual implementation is required, what is the correct way to implement UPDATE_ENTITY_LIST in SAP RAP Managed Scenario?

    • Should it be placed in the Behavior Implementation class, or does it require a different CDS or custom interface?

  3. Are there specific configurations in the Behavior Definition or OData service that I might have missed?

    • For example, does the Delta Payload or the @context annotation in the request need specific settings?

I would appreciate guidance or examples to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: DDMRP - PIR is considered in one Plant and not in other
entry.content:
This Issue was resolved. There were two reasons:

1. In the MRP3 view, the Period indicator in Forecast was not maintained. Once it was maintained, the system considered PIR to arrive at ADU. (I checked in two different systems, for consistency with many materials)

2. Even if the period indicator was maintained, we could not see the PIR. The reason was the y-axis scale of the ADU graph. Since past consumption was high and ADU was also high, the system considered that the scale was high. Future PIRs showed bars with very few heights, which were almost invisible. Once we opened the details through the table, they were there. 

S. Sathiyamurthy

entry.title: Collecting data for SAP SCT Metric Weight of Material Used: Hazard class of a material
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I’m looking for help identifying a table that links hazard classes to materials in S/4HANA. Specifically, I need to find hazard classifications (e.g., according to GHS or SARA) associated with a material (MATNR) or hazardous material number (STOFF).

Here’s what I’ve explored so far:

  • I found the table MGEF, which has fields STOFF (Hazardous Material Number) and LAGKL (Storage Class). However, the values of LAGKL don’t match the hazard classes required for the Weight of Material Used metric in SAP SCT (Sustainability Control Tower).
  • I also found the tables EHFNDC_CHCLASS (Classification of Chemicals according to GHS) and EHFNDD_CHM_SHCLS (Classification of Chemicals according to SARA). These tables contain hazard classes that are more aligned with my needs.

My question: Is there a table or mechanism in SAP S/4HANA that links STOFF or MATNR to hazard classes according to GHS or SARA? 

Cheers,
Alina

entry.title: Currency Handling in SAP for Multi-Plant or multi-country Operations
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a requirement in the current project where different plants are located in US and Europe. While the local currencies will be recorded differently, the final reporting needs to be in USD

Key Requirement:

  • Europe plants will record transactions in EUR (their local currency).
  • US plants will record transactions in USD (local currency).
  • The system should automatically convert all transactions to USD for reporting purposes.

Proposed Solution:

  1. Setup Local Currencies:

    • Assign the relevant local currencies to each plant’s company code:
      • US Plants: USD (Local Currency).
      • Europe Plants: EUR (Local Currency).

  2. Enable Multiple Currencies in the Ledger:

    • Use parallel currencies functionality in SAP S/4HANA to manage different currencies.
    • Configuration Path:
      SPRO > Financial Accounting (New) > Financial Accounting Global Settings > Ledgers > Define Settings for Ledgers and Currency Types.
    • For the primary ledger:
      • Local Currency (Currency Type 10): EUR for Europe, USD for US.
      • Group Currency (Currency Type 30): Set to USD for consolidated reporting.

  3. Maintain Exchange Rates:

    • Maintain the EUR → USD exchange rates in SAP (OB08) for accurate conversion.
    • SAP will automatically apply these rates when posting transactions.

  4. Document Currency Handling:

    • Post transactions in local currency (EUR/USD) depending on the plant.
    • The system will automatically translate these into USD based on the maintained exchange rates for reporting purposes.

  5. Reporting in Group Currency:

    • Use reports like:
      • FAGLL03H (G/L Account Balance in Group Currency).
      • FBL1N/FBL3N (Vendor/GL Line Item Display with currency selection).
      • S_ALR_87012284 (Trial Balance with group currency view).

Example Scenario:

  • A Europe plant posts an invoice of €1,000.
  • SAP converts this to $1,200 (assuming 1 EUR = 1.2 USD).
  • The report will show $1,200 in group currency (USD), aligning with the final reporting requirement.

Let me know if there are further clarifications or additional configurations needed.

Best regards,
Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: Antrhopic released a protocol for AI Agents and Tools based on a Client/Server architecture
entry.content:
ODBC, HTTP, or SOA, but for AI. And brought to you by the #1 model for Instruction following and Tool Calling provider.

Anthropic releases a toolkit for connecting large language models to external systems, they have called it Model Context Protocol, or MCP for short, and is available under an open-source license.

The problem they are trying to solve

No standard way of connecting data sources to models exists just yet; this decision is left to enterprise users and model and database providers.

Today, developers write a specific Python code in LangChain or LlamaIndex, pointing LLMs to databases. With each LLM functioning a little differently from each other, developers need a separate code for each one to connect to specific data sources. This often results in different models calling to the same databases without the ability to work together seamlessly. 

 

The protocol aims to eliminate the need for developers to write redundant custom integration code every time they need to link a new tool or data source to an AI system. Instead, MCP provides a unified method for all these connections, allowing developers to spend more time building features and less time on integration. To illustrate MCP’s broad utility, imagine AI models needing to interact with different types of data: from PostgreSQL databases to cloud platforms like Google Drive or Slack. MCP’s goal is to streamline these interactions, reducing the manual coding that’s currently necessary.

The interaction between AI agents and web services is becoming increasingly complex, as current methods like web scraping and simulated browser interactions have significant challenges. Web scraping suffers from issues such as inconsistent data extraction due to changing HTML structures and potential legal and ethical violations, while simulated browser interactions are resource-intensive and struggle with dynamic content.

Integration is also really comlplicated due to a lack of standardization in APIs and data formats.

 

Anthropic wants to solve this, instead offering a built-in search or similar tool, Anthropic brings an open-source protocol. This protocol it’s not just for Anthropic’s models, but for any model, enabling a plug-and-play ecosystem where tools and LLMs can be swapped with ease.

The MCP works as a two-way bridge between data sources and tools. For now, the Claude desktop app acts as the host, but it’s easy to imagine integrations expanding to platforms like VS Code or other productivity tools. The protocol lets the LLM fetch data from external sources and even send back outputs, opening the door to a multitude of use cases from weather queries to dynamic code editing directly in an IDE such as Cursor or VS Code.

Anthropic provides array of pre-built MCP servers Anthropic offers, covering GitHub integrations, file systems, scraping tools, and others. They’ve also provided SDKs in TypeScript and Python, empowering developers to create custom servers tailored to specific needs. The protocol currently focuses on local integrations through stdio, though there are discussions about supporting additional transport methods like SSE and WebSockets. 

This means that initially, MCP focuses on local connections, meaning the server runs on the same machine as the client.

The concept of LLMs using external protocols isn’t new. Take ChatGPT plugins from March 2023, which aimed to enable LLMs to access external resources like Bing search or other tools. While innovative, it didn't take off. Anthropic, however, seems to have approached this differently.

Core MCP Features

MCP aims to solve the N×M integration problem between LLM applications and context providers by standardizing how LLMs interact with external tools and data sources.

In the community, the NxM problem is defined as having M different prompts for N different llms.

The protocol provides three main primitives:
- Tool calling for executing functions
- Resources for accessing read-only data
- Prompts for standardized interactions

 

 

There is a human in the loop because, at the moment, only Claude Desktop supports MCP, First step would be to go through the MCP documentation, starting with the Getting Started guide. or Glama.ai guide. I am familiar with using Claude via AWS Bedrock, so planned on integrating directly there, but, again, MCP is only supported on Claude desktop as of today, apparently, the idea will be to extend it, and soon be able to have generation and tool use contained within a customer’s AWS account using bedrock, but yet to see. Careful here!

 

Remains unknown if other LLM providers will adopt this tool protocol, at the time of this blog, there is one main alternative to the MCP for connecting AI models with external data and tools called UIM (Unified Intent Mediator) Protocol.

The key difference between MCP and UIM is while MCP focuses on providing a standardized protocol for two-way connections, the UIM Protocol takes a different approach by emphasizing intent-based interactions and ethical considerations in AI-service communications. It is quite complicated that protocol.

For enterprise teams considering MCP, the current iteration is best used as a prototyping tool. MCPs' success will depend on the adoption of the major industry players, like Google, Amazon, Microsoft, or OpenAI. 

I hope if this becomes mainstream, we could have our own artifacts built for our local and cloud services, so we could have these artifacts running on kubernetes, interacting with other cloud services and LLMs (Visionary mode)

Other important question I have since I got to it is (if they release it apart from Claude Desktop) choosing between building a tool using the MCP protocol or LangChain, where some of us have multiple tools already defined.

MCP is more flexible and lies in a lower level than LangChain. MCP might be better if you want more control over the integration layer and prefer to align closely with open standards rather than relying on a library like LangChain.

But, LangChain provides out-of-the-box connectors to various data sources (databases, APIs, document loaders) and it allows quick experimentation, it is not tied to the underlying infrastructure. The protocol currently is limited to the protocol where the same Client and Server is located, in the current case, a Desktop.

Also, LangChain offers a thriving community, and many utilities for memory, chaining prompts, and reasoning. Its not for the developer to be managing protocols and standards, but makes the development suitable only for the library which is built for.

But, for large enterprise vendors, such as SAP, a protocol is what is needed and here is where I believe that the original intent of OpenAI was, to provide open standards and protocols, so kudos to Anthropic I hope it takes off.

entry.title: Streamlining SAC Story Publication: Automating PDF Exports with IPS Transformations
entry.content:
Streamlining SAC Story Publication with IPS Transformation: Generating PDFs

As organizations increasingly rely on SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for data-driven insights, the ability to share SAC stories in a simplified and consistent format is essential. One of the highly sought-after capabilities in SAC is publishing stories as PDFs, which makes distributing dashboards and reports easy. However, aligning this feature with custom user group setups often requires adjustments to synchronization processes.

In this blog, I will discuss the use of SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) transformations to improve the SAC story publication process and streamline PDF generation for custom user groups. This involves a step-by-step guide to setting up the IPS transformations, ensuring efficient access management, and how it enables smooth and automated PDF publication.

Overview of SAC Story Publication and IPS Transformation

SAP Analytics Cloud allows users to schedule the export of stories in various formats, including PDF. This is critical for teams that need to share reports regularly, ensuring that all stakeholders receive data in a format that is easy to digest.

However, for teams using SAP Incentive Management or other modules with custom user groups, configuring SAC to publish stories and allow specific access requires robust user synchronization between SAC and custom groups. SAP’s Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) comes into play here, offering a streamlined solution to maintain user permissions and automate the export of stories.

Why IPS Transformation Matters

For many users, setting up IPS transformations ensures proper mapping between user groups in SAP systems, such as SAP Incentive Management, and the corresponding teams in SAC. This is essential because:

  1. Custom Access Control: Users in specific groups need access to stories, but not all teams have identical access requirements.
  2. Automated Synchronization: Manually managing user permissions is impractical for large organizations. IPS ensures that all users have the correct access levels in SAC.
  3. Simplifying Story Export: With IPS transformations, you can schedule the automatic generation of PDF versions of stories for appropriate users based on their group membership, ensuring no one misses vital reports.

Implementing IPS Transformation for SAC Story PDF Publication

Here’s a high-level overview of the steps needed to configure IPS transformations to support SAC story publication:

Step 1: Define User Groups in SAP Incentive Management

To begin, ensure that custom user groups are defined in SAP Incentive Management. These groups will determine which users can access specific SAC stories. Make sure these groups reflect the reporting and publishing needs of your organization.

Step 2: Configure IPS Transformations

SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) allows for the transformation of user data during the synchronization process. This ensures that user groups in SAP Incentive Management are correctly mapped to SAC, providing users with the right permissions for story access.

You will need to:

  • Set up an IPS job to synchronize user groups between SAP Incentive Management and SAC.
  • Apply transformations to filter out unnecessary users and include only those relevant to the SAC stories.

IPS Transformation Code for Getting the Report in PDF format to the End user.

{
    "sourcePath": "$.userName",
    "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:user-custom-parameters:1.0']['extensionProperty1']"
}

Note :Once the above changes are done to the transformations run the Sync job to

Step 3: Schedule Story Publications in SAC

Once IPS transformations are set, you can configure SAC to automatically export stories in PDF format based on the user groups. Navigate to the Story Publishing feature in SAC and:

  • Schedule stories for publication.
  • Choose the PDF format.
  • Select the custom user groups as recipients of these PDFs.

This automated scheduling ensures that the right users receive updated stories based on their group membership.

Step 4: Automate PDF Distribution

The final step is to automate the distribution of the PDFs. IPS transformations ensure that the correct users are synchronized with SAC, and scheduled exports mean that users receive stories without manual intervention. If users move between groups, the IPS job will automatically adjust their permissions and ensure they receive the appropriate reports.

The Benefits of Automating SAC Story Publication via IPS

  1. Improved Accuracy: Synchronizing user groups with IPS ensures that the right users always have access to the correct reports.
  2. Efficiency: Manual handling of report distribution is time-consuming. Automating this process with IPS and SAC saves both time and effort.
  3. Scalability: This setup is ideal for organizations with a large number of users and complex reporting requirements. IPS transformations help scale the solution without adding complexity to user management.

Conclusion

The combination of IPS transformations and SAC’s story publishing capabilities enables organizations to streamline the distribution of critical business reports. By automating user synchronization and ensuring timely PDF exports of SAC stories, teams can focus on analysis rather than manual report generation. This process guarantees that the right stakeholders always receive the insights they need, aligned with their access permissions.

#SLIThoughtLeadership

entry.title: Re: SAP CAPM service failing with http 407 Proxy Authenti...
entry.content:
Usually, when "it" sometimes works and sometimes fails, this is due to "load-balancing". I.e., there are two systems involved, one where the configuration is correct, and another one, where it isn't (e.g. in this case: wrong proxy password maintained). And the client (or some other component inbetween) then makes some kind of load-balancing and sometimes hits the working one, and other times the non-working one.

I know, this is only "very general", but there are not many details of the entire scenario/landscape available here. So try investigating into that direction and check, whether there may be any component involved here, of which there are two or more instances.

entry.title: Re: Launch Excel SAC Add-In file from SAC folder
entry.content:
@MelCalucin 

Currently you can only launch Excel templates from Excel interface by browsing the location on the tenant. So if you sign in to Excel SAC and go Open>Browse the tenant folder where Excel files were saved, this will launch the excel report. But if you do the other way round and go to the SAC tenant folder and click on the Excel file, it will get downloaded as excel and then you have to open it and logon to it for further use. Option to directly launch the Excel files from SAC stories is a feature planned for 2025Q1 as per the roadmap.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Enable Now as ChatBot resource
entry.content:
Hi Marie,

Sorry, I don't know how to configure you Chatbot but every Enable Now object has a unique URL so is you can use that information to configure your Chatbot then you are good to go.  To access that unique URL:

1. Open Manager and open the Workarea containing the content that is to be linked into your Chatbot
2. Click on the down arrow beside the All Content link and select Published (top, right corner)
3. Click on a content object.  The Start Link attribute contains the unique URL that can be used to access that content object

Shane

entry.title: Use SAP BW query conditions to create measure-based filters in SAC stories (Live Connection)
entry.content:
Introduction

Last week, I had insightful discussions with colleagues and a customer using SAC live on top of SAP BW/4HANA. The topic was whether conditions from SAP BW queries could be leveraged to create measure-based filters in SAP Analytics Cloud stories based on live models. That conversation inspired this blog.

📌Highlights in the blog:
• Learn about SAP BW conditions and how SAC supports them
• A step-by-step guide to creating conditions in SAP BW queries
• How to make the process dynamic with variables
• Using those variables in SAC story filters
• Leveraging those variables for slider filters in SAC

Let's start 

In one of my recent blog posts, I presented a method for creating a What-If Simulation in an SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Story using an SAC Live Model (with SAP BW/4HANA as an example). For more details, please refer to the relevant blog here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-if-simulation-in-a-sac-story-based-on-a-sac-live-model-e-g-sap-bw/bc-p/13525488

If you want to see how this looks when you follow the steps outlined in the mentioned blog, please watch this sneak peek:

While using acquired data, you can create measure-based filters in SAP Analytics Cloud; however, this is currently not available when using a live connection with SAP BW as the data source. Of course, you can use scripting to filter a specific table or chart, but I prefer a solution that works across all SAP Analytics Cloud stories by modifying the underlying SAP BW Query just once. If there is a need to have this functionality only in a few SAC dashboards, you can just follow my way.

We will leverage an SAP BW Query condition combined with an SAP BW Query variable, making the condition much more flexible and reusable in various scenarios. In other words, we will create a single formula variable on the SAP BW side that can be used universally. The good news is that it’s simpler than it sounds!🙂

Now let's start

The following steps provide a general explanation of what conditions are. We will first learn the basics. Later, we will explore how to leverage some 'flexibility' in their usage - what is the main purpose of this blog.

First we need a SAP BW Query which holds some measures (key figures) and characteristics (dimensions).
And then we will add a condition to this SAP BW query. Let's start first with the condition itself and what it does. Generally spoken you can define conditions for queries so that you can hide irrelevant figures. The definition of this condition happens directly in the SAP BW Query. 

But what is a condition in a SAP BW Query? To improve the efficiency of data analysis, you can formulate conditions. In the results area of the query, the data is filtered according to the conditions so that only the part of the results area that you are interested in is displayed.

If you apply conditions to a query, you are not changing any figures; you are just hiding the numbers that are not relevant for you. Conditions therefore have no effect on the values displayed in the results rows. The results row of a query with an active condition is the same as the results row of a query without this condition.

You can define multiple conditions for a query. Conditions are evaluated independently of each other. The result set is therefore independent of the evaluation sequence. The result is the intersection of the individual conditions. Multiple conditions are linked logically with AND. A characteristic value is only displayed when it fulfills all (active) conditions of the query (seeLinking Two Conditions: Bottom 2 Revenue and Invoiced Quantity > 100,000,000).

You can choose from the following operators for threshold value conditions:

  • = Equal To
  • <> Not Equal To
  • > Greater Than
  • >= Greater Than or Equal To
  • < Less Than
  • <= Less Than or Equal To
  • [] Between
  • ][ Not Between

The values for these operators must be floating point numbers. For the operators [] Between and ][ Not Between, you must enter a value range, that is, an upper and a lower threshold value.

You can choose from the following operators for ranked list conditions:

  • Top N
  • Top %
  • Top Total
  • Bottom N
  • Bottom %
  • Bottom Total

We will go with the "> Greater Than" condition in our example. It acts like a "Threshold Value Condition": An entry is filtered independently of the other entries if its reference value has a specific relationship to a comparison value. For example, an entry is not displayed if its reference value exceeds or is less than a specific threshold value (for example, gross revenue > 500000).

SAP Analytics Cloud can handle conditions originating from SAP BW Queries. If a specific condition from a SAP BW Query is used across multiple live models or if several SAP Analytics Cloud stories rely on a live model built on top of a SAP BW Query with a condition, they can all utilize it. This eliminates the need to create multiple conditions. But that's a different story.

Let's start with a SAP BW Query with some key figures (measures). We can make the conditions flexible, but I will make it simple so anyone can follow. In this case lets focus on the "Gross Margin" where we want to see only values above . We will click on the "Conditions" tab to proceed.

In the next screen we will see that there is no condition available. Let's create one. In the conditions tab perform a right-click and select "New Condition"':

Now click on "Add".


We will select a key figure (measure), in our example the "Gross Margin", and will select the Comparison Operator "GreaterThan".

Now we need a value. I will use "100000" and hit "OK".

Our condition in the BW Query is ready now. I have also added a description. If I save the SAP BW Query, we can continue.

We can do much more here, but let's focus on the main topic. Before introducing flexibility, we need to understand what happens in SAP Analytics Cloud (or in other SAP BI clients, such as SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office or the SAC Add-in for MS Excel)

Now, if I build a SAP Analytics Cloud live model on top of this SAP BW Query, I can create my SAC story and will be able to activate or deactivate this condition in the tables.....

....or charts that use this SAC live model. In the following chart the condition is active.

This is how it looks when the condition is inactive:

The following short animation demonstrates this nice interaction which works automatically plug & play as SAP Analytics Cloud just leverage the features coming from the SAP BW Query. When the condition is enabled, only values for the Gross Margin above 100K are displayed. When the condition is disabled, all values for the Gross Margin are visible.

So far so good, but now bring some flexibility into this condition on SAP BW Query and on SAP Analytics Cloud side to get our measure based filter.

Bring flexibility into the process and get our measure based filter

Let's return to the condition in our SAP BW Query to make a change. We go to the conditions tab and klick on "Edit" next to the condition.

Next to the value field, you will notice a small symbol, which is the standard symbol for variables in SAP BW Queries. Just click on it.


Clicking on this symbol opens a selection screen where you can choose from the available variables.
I have already created a "formula variable" wich will help us but how we can create our own variable?

To create our own, we need to do this directly at the SAP BW Query level. This can be done in various places. Typically, you need to right-click on the SAP BW Query and select "Variable". You will also notice the symbol we already saw in the SAP BW Query.

In the next window enter a name (usually it starts with Z or whatever name space is allowed in your system) and a description. Follow now with the variable type. THIS IS NOW IMPORTANT: You must select "FORMULA" here as we need a formula variable which stores a number (our threshold for the Gross Margin). If your are done, click on "finish".

Now we have our formula variable ready to use. Let's return to our SAP BW Query.
Navigate to the "Conditions" tab, open the condition by selecting "Edit", choose the newly created condition, and click "OK".

If you now run the SAC story using this SAP BW Query, you will notice a new prompt. This is our newly created SAP BW variable. Currently, it defaults to a value of '0.' Of course, within the variable settings, you can also define a default value (e.g., 250,000), but I did not set one. This is what it looks like:

In my example I have entered "200000" and if I hit "Set" I can see the result.
Now I can only see values above 200000. 

But how we can add this as a filter into the story?

If you followed my SAP Analytics Cloud Live Blog ( https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/analyse-your-data-live-with-sap-analytics-cloud-on-top-of-sap-bw-4hana-sap/ba-p/13933035 ), you will know that we can use variables from the SAP BW as Query in the filter dialog.

Let's see how this works. We open our SAC Story in the "edit" mode. In the next step we activate the "Filter Panel"

Make sure that you select the right SAP Analytics Cloud live model. 
After that select the screen with the "Variables".

In the last step, select your variable you have created in the SAP BW Query and which we saw also in the variable prompt.

Now save the SAC Story and run it in the view mode. You will now notice the variable from the SAP BW Query, directly in the filter panel of the story. We can also show all other variables there but let's focus on the main topic of the blog again.

Now we can enter a value and see what happens on the screen. If I enter 200000, I will only see all Gross Margin values above this value:
If I enter 100000, I will only see all Gross Margin values above this value:

The following animation shows how this works.

But what about more flexibility? What if we use a slider? Let's see how we can achieve this.
First we need a script variable which should store the value for our threshold.

Let's give it a name and a description. We need to select "number" as type.

Finally we click on "Done" and leave this window.
In the next step we include a slider into our SAC Story.

I set the background of the slider to white and used the following settings.
Make sure that you select the right variable coming from the SAP BW Query and the right Script Variable.
Some selections are optional.

Close the Designer on the right side and let's add a button. 

If needed add a text and a tool tip in the settings of the button.

Now let's add a little bit scripting to the button.

I will use this peace of code:

Chart_C1.getDataSource().setVariableValue("ZCONDITIONADEM",MyTresholdSAC);
Table_C1.getDataSource().setVariableValue("ZCONDITIONADEM",MyTresholdSAC);

Description of the used ID's:

There is no need to write down all the ID's.
The ID's from the charts & tables you will get from the settings of each widget.
Also the SAP BW Variable and the Script Variable can be easily selected. 
Just hit the "STRG" and "SPACE" keys on your keyboard.

And now we are done. Let's see what happens.
If I set the threshold to 20000 and hit the filter button it will filter my Gross Margin.

You can change the maximum value in the settings of the slide. I did it and changed it to 250000.
The following animation shows a few slides and clicks.

I’m quite sure you can achieve all of this using scripting in the SAC story, and there are other methods available depending on the data source. However, in this case, I wanted to demonstrate a flexible approach when using SAP BW as the data source, especially if there’s a need for a measure filter like this based on a live model. While there is currently an influence request to make this functionality native in SAC available for live models, this workaround is available today.

I also believe there is room for optimization. The goal here is simply to show what is possible with minimal effort. Normally, everything I’ve demonstrated in this blog can be implemented in a very short time. You might need assistance from an SAP BW Query expert, but once the SAP BW Query is ready, the rest is quite straightforward.

The blog showcases only two filtering methods, but of course, there are more (e.g. range filters, other filter types).🙂

I hope you enjoyed this blog! 

Best regards.

Adem

entry.title: Re: Data Services job scheduler not running when specifyi...
entry.content:
We ran into the same problem and tried a community solution works.

Solution:

1. go into the scheduler folder on your server Win server will be in C:\ProgramData\SAP BusinessObjects\Data Services\log\{Job Server}\Schedules\{local job server user}

2. append the end of the txt file of the scheduler with: -Q"LOCAL_REPO_SERVER_NAME"

Referenced solution:

Solved: SAP Data Services Job Scheduler is not working not... - SAP Community

With the newly designed Cloud tab in Finance & Legal dashboard within SAP for Me, Customers can now initiate and manage Full Cloud Contract Terminations (covering all items on the order form) using the "Terminate Contract" option under the new "Manage Contract".

entry.title: Cancelling a backup session in HANA
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We have HANA databases running in our production environment and the database backups are running in the Cohesity(3rd party). Sometimes we are observing the DB backups that going to hung status, during this time we are trying to cancel the hung backup from both HANA DB side and from Cohesity. We will follow the SAP note "2827950 - How-To: Cancel Hanging Backup on SAP HANA"  , most of the times that options which SAP has provided in note will work. And only restart is option to cancel the hung backup.  

In this situation, when the issue occurs in production DB, it is impossible restart the production DB during the business days. If we don't restart the DB the backup's will not happen in production system. Could you please check and help us to find a alternative solution to cancel the backup without DB restart.

DB version: 2.00.072


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cancelling a backup session in HANA
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To resolve the issue you are facing, you can also try terminating the backup session by referring to KBA 2092196 - How-To: Terminating Sessions in SAP HANA. 
However, it is possible that none of the approaches mentioned in the article will work if you have already executed the 'BACKUP CANCEL' command and the backup session is still active. 
If you encounter this situation, the only option available is to restart the HANA database as described also in KBA 3423178 - How can a hanging backup session be canceled with the Alert-ID: 65 for long-running backups?. 

If you come across this issue, it is important to collect runtimedumps (three pieces with a 1-minute interval as mentioned in note 1813020) while the issue is occurring. 
These runtimedumps will help us analyze the problem and provide you with the root cause and resolution to prevent the HANA database from encountering the same situation again. 

Additionally, I strongly recommend keeping the HANA database updated to the latest revision whenever possible. 
Considering that the latest revisions are 2.00.059.15 (SPS05)/2.00.079.01 (SPS07)/2.00.080 (SPS08), the current HANA revision is quite outdated for a production system. 
Please consider upgrading the HANA database as soon as possible to minimize potential risks associated with this revision.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: How to create many approvers in workflow that is created by SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi all,

I would like to create the Approval process with many approvers in the workflow that is created by SAP Build Process Automation.

The Approval process I would like to get is that

if price > 100, go to Approver A

else if price >= 100 <= 300, go to Approver A, if Approver A approved, go to Approver B

else if price > 300, go to Approver C

So, the corresponding approval request should be sent to the corresponding Approver's Inbox only and other approvers should not see it.

 

For now, I created all approver users under my BTP account's Security > Users.

So, all approver users share to use only one Inbox and all approval requests sent from Workflow of SAP Build Process Automation can be able to see by all approvers.

 

Is it possible to show the corresponding request only for the corresponding approver by creating users like this?

If yes, which step I need to add to get the requirement?

(or)

If not, could you please guide me how can I get this kind of Approval process?

 

Thank you.

Best Regards,

Aye Chan

entry.title: Re: Question regarding Data Source Integration in Content Form Templates
entry.content:
Hi Prasanth @PrasanthPadmanabhan

As additional questions:
1. Is it possible to output custom business objects (custom tables) to forms as well?
2. When outputting data to forms, is the process correct where data flows from CDS views/tables through custom logic to custom fields in transaction documents? In other words, if the form output application doesn't support custom fields/custom logic, is my understanding correct that such implementation would be impossible?
3. If there are other methods to output fields not present in standard form data sources to forms, please let me know.

BR,
K

entry.title: Unable to see Manage Departments in User Management
entry.content:
Hi SAP,


I am unable to see "Manage Departments" in User Management even though my S-User ID is an Administrator.


Please help to check. 
Thank you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to see Manage Departments in User Management
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

To locate Manage Departments, please follow the steps below:

  1. Go to User Management
  2. Click Manage Services top right.
  3. In the drop-down choose Manage Departments.
  • Click + to add a new department. Type the department name and click Save.
  • Click the red trash can icon next to the department you wish to delete (then click OK to finalize).
  • Click edit (pencil icon) to edit an existing entry and click Save.

If you wish to Manage (add/remove) existing departments for S-users, follow these steps:

  1. In User Management, click the Users tab
  2. Select the S-user(s) you wish to maintain
  3. Click Change Department at the top of the Users list
  4. The Change Department screen will pop up
  • Click a Department in the list to assign it, then click the Assign confirmation button
  • Click Remove Department to delete the assigned department and click the Remove confirmation button

For more detailed information, please refer to KBA: 2361192 - How to manage department information for S-user IDs - SAP for Me

Hope the above information is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Judy

entry.title: How to change System Role in SAP for Me portal
entry.content:
In sap.me portal the system is shown with System Role "Test". We would like to use this system as "Development" System Role. Are there any changes/restrictions after the System role change to us?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to change System Role in SAP for Me portal
entry.content:
Can the System Type be changed for existing systems?

Please follow the Best Practices articles when editing existing systems:
KBA 2531837 – Best practices of editing SAP Business Suite systems and license keys - SAP for Me

What types of changes should not (or cannot) be made to an existing systems or license keys?
If you need to change any of the following, you must create a new system with new license keys in the License Keys application. So in cases where you have a new deployment of the software, you should create a new system instead of editing an existing system. Please do not edit an existing system or license key for the following types of changes:

  • Product Name and/or Version changed  
  • Operating System changed
  • System Type or Development Phase changed (e.g. License Type changes from Test, Development, Production)
  • Database changed - Note: As mentioned above, only change the database for an existing system if all license keys under that system are to be changed to the new database. If you plan to use the old database for some systems and new data for others, you should create separate systems for each database. 
  • 3-character System ID cannot be changed (a new system needs to be created)


KBA 2446681 - Best practices of updating BOBJ license keys & system data - SAP for Me

  • System type (production, development, test) can be changed but changing the system type by editing an existing system, could have the unintended consequences of overdrawing your license entitlement. This is why we recommend creating a new system when the system type changes.

entry.title: Cannot use 'BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CHAGE'

It can be changed before upgrading

entry.title: Introducing Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Monitoring in PP/DS for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Background

SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for planning and scheduling (PP/DS) capability is used to create procurement proposals for in-house production or external procurement to cover product requirements and to optimize and plan the resource schedule and the order dates/times in detail. PP/DS helps a Production Planner to reduce the lead times, to increase on-time delivery performance, or to increase the throughput of products and reduce the stock costs.

However, how would the Production Planner know whether their production plan is really helping them achieve their goals or not?

Depending on the complexity of the business, a production planner could be dealing with hundreds of resources, thousands of products, and thousands of partners and customers. Monitoring the Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for complex production plans is critical to ensure that the production plan is feasible and is aligned with business goals.

 

Introduction

As part of the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) release 2024.Q4, a new Business Content Package for Line of Business (LoB) titled "Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling" is released. This Business Content Package includes 4 stories -

  • Order Details
  • Order Operations
  • Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand)
  • Resource Capacity

The above SAC stories need a live data connection to SAP S/4HANA 2023 system. The four Analytical Queries released as part of SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 serve as the data source for these stories

Once you have successfully created the connection between your SAC tenant and your SAP S/4HANA system, the SAC stories would display the KPIs and charts based on your data.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) stories

Order Details

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Order Details story has an Overview and a Details page, and displays important performance indicators about your orders, such as the:

  • number of orders
  • number of delayed orders
  • lead, processing, queue, setup, and wait time
  • number of schedule lines
  • confirmed, original, receipt, scrap, and total quantity

Figure 1: Order Details - OverviewFigure 2: Order Details - Details

This story also links to and from the Order Operations and Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) stories.

Order Operations

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Order Operations story has an Overview page and displays important performance indicators about the operations of your orders, such as the:

  • number of operations
  • number of sub operations
  • gross operation duration
  • net operation duration

Figure 3: Order Operations - Overview

This story also links to and from the Order Details and Resource Capacity stories.

Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand)

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) story has an Overview and a Details page, and displays important performance indicators about your orders and their pegging relationships, such as the:

  • service level percentage
  • maximum earliness and lateness time
  • total buffer time
  • early, on-time, delayed, and total relationships count
  • requirement quantity

Figure 4: Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) - OverviewFigure 5: Pegging Relationships (Order to Demand) - Details

This story also links to and from the Order Details story.

Resource Capacity

The SAP PP/DS: KPI Monitoring - Resource Capacity story has an Overview and a Details page, and displays important performance indicators about your resources, such as the:

  • number of overloaded resources
  • capacity utilization
  • capacity required for setup, teardown, production, and queue

Figure 6: Resource Capacity - OverviewFigure 7: Resource Capacity - Details

This story also links to and from the Order Operations story.

 

Wrapping Up

The SAC stories for KPI Monitoring in PP/DS can be used to track and monitor the KPIs based on your current production plan. You can use these SAC stories "as is", or by fine-tuning them or by using them as reference to create your own SAC stories. 

Please feel free to share your feedback and comments.

P.S.: Refer SAP Note 3495936 - Restrictions and Implementation Recommendations for Analytics in PP/DS on SAP S/4HANA 2023 to ensure all the pre-requisites are maintained in your SAP S/4HANA system.

entry.title: Analytical Insights in Joule (powered by SAC), Controlled release in Q1 2025
entry.content:
Introduction

We’re excited to announce Analytical Insights in Joule, powered by SAP Analytics Cloud with the upcoming QRC1.2025 release of SAP Analytics Cloud. This post provides an overview of the capability, its current scope, future directions, prerequisites, and release plans.

Analytics Insights in Joule – Overview and Functionality

What are Analytical Insights in Joule?

Today, Joule can be enabled in many SAP applications like SuccessFactors, S4HANA etc. In case you are not aware and want to get started with Joule, refer this page.

If you have Joule already enabled in any of your SAP applications, then analytical capability in Joule, powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, allows business users to explore their data and gain insights into your business, in a natural, conversational experience. Using familiar business terms, simply ask your question, and Joule will instantly provide answers as simple charts and metrics.

Joule works with data from models that are indexed by the Just Ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud. This means that the administrator for Just Ask in the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant in your SAP BTP landscape determines what models, and therefore data, are available to in Joule once the analytical capability is enabled.

What is in the controlled release? (incl. limitations)

In the first release of SAP Analytics Cloud and Joule integration, any user who has access to Joule and has a valid user in SAP Analytics enterprise tenant can access this capability in Joule.  Further, your analytical universe in SAP analytics cloud can only consist of Acquired/Imported SAC models specifically enabled by user.

With the first release, you will be able to realize the following benefits:

  • Ask analytical questions naturally in your language via Joule (which can soon be enabled in your cloud application, if not already is)
  • You don’t have to login to SAC to get analytical insights, get them from the context of your business application

Note the following limitations exist during controlled release. We will try to lift them as soon as possible in the upcoming releases:

  • Joule itself cannot yet be enabled in SAC enterprise tenant (this is work in progress and you can expect it  soon).
  • Currently, only acquired (imported) models are supported. No live data models are supported.
  • Analytical Insights can only be metrices and charts.
  • The analytical capability is only supported when the Joule client interface is in U.S. English.
  • Google Chrome and New Microsoft Edge browsers are supported.

Infrastructure & Prerequisites

What are the prerequisites to access this capability?

Following are the set of pre-requisites for this capability to be available:

  • You should have access to Joule in one or more of SAP applications
  • You should have SAP analytics cloud in your landscape
  • A user can access this capability only if has a valid user in SAP Analytics Cloud

Controlled release & Way forward

Who can participate in the Controlled Release?

You must be a customer of both SAP Analytics Cloud and any of SAP cloud application that has Joule enabled. Your SAP analytics cloud tenant must be running in Cloud Foundry, NEO landscape is not planned to be supported.

How can I sign up?

Registration page is available on Customer Influence Portal.

When can I expect general availability?

General availability is planned for QRC2.2025. Note that availability only applies to SAP Analytics Cloud tenants running on Cloud Foundry.

Conclusion

We are looking forward to welcoming customers to this controlled release and to make this capability available to more and more customers in 2025. We shall deliver additional functionality unlocking more of the potential of this incredibly powerful integration.

entry.title: How to perform foreign currency valuation using functional currency for selected vendor or customers
entry.content:
In the "Perform Foreign Currency Valuation" tile, valuation posting is based on company code currency. Notice that there is Advanced Foreign Currency Valuations using "Schedule General Ledger Jobs" tile to perform valuation on functional currency (Perform Foreign Currency Valuation with the new Advanced Valuations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud). However, it doesn't allow users to select specific vendors or customers, meaning that all AR and AP items will be included in foreign currency valuation posting.

May I check how to cater both requirements?

  1. use functional currency for foreign currency valuation
  2. allow users to select specific vendors or customers for foreign currency valuation
